text: People are tired, self-treating, and under-informed
textPreview: People are tired, self-treating, and under-informed

text: Sleep aid use is common enough to support a focused evidence product.
textPreview: Sleep aid use is common enough to support a focused evidence product.

text: SOMNARY STRATEGY
textPreview: SOMNARY STRATEGY

text: The category has a trust gap, not just an information gap
textPreview: The category has a trust gap, not just an information gap

text: The best wedge is where consumer behavior, product-label weakness, and safety uncertainty collide.
textPreview: The best wedge is where consumer behavior, product-label weakness, and safety uncertainty collide.

text: Melatonin use rose from 0.4% to 2.1% of US adults from 1999-2000 to 2017-2018.
textPreview: Melatonin use rose from 0.4% to 2.1% of US adults from 1999-2000 to 2017-2018.

text: JAMA found melatonin gummies ranged from 74% to 347% of the labeled amount.
textPreview: JAMA found melatonin gummies ranged from 74% to 347% of the labeled amount.

text: NCCIH says short-term melatonin appears relatively safe, but long-term safety is not established.
textPreview: NCCIH says short-term melatonin appears relatively safe, but long-term safety is not established.

text: CBT-I has first-line guideline support, but supplement marketing dominates the consumer conversation.
textPreview: CBT-I has first-line guideline support, but supplement marketing dominates the consumer conversation.

text: Implication: Somnary should sell clarity before it sells features.
textPreview: Implication: Somnary should sell clarity before it sells features.
